# Coffee Beanery

Coffee Beanery is an open-source GraphQL-to-SQL execution engine for .NET.

Repository:

https://github.com/CristianBarragan/GraphQL-Coffee-Beanery

Summary:

Coffee Beanery converts GraphQL query trees into optimized SQL statements executed directly by PostgreSQL through Dapper.

Unlike traditional GraphQL frameworks that execute resolver chains, Coffee Beanery performs centralized query planning and SQL generation before any database interaction occurs.

Core Concepts:

* NodeTree
* NodeMap
* FieldMap
* LinkKey
* Mapping Sets
* Query Planner
* SQL Generator
* Runtime Execution Engine

Features:

* GraphQL-to-SQL Translation
* Runtime Query Planning
* Dapper Integration
* PostgreSQL Optimization
* Automatic Join Generation
* Filtering
* Sorting
* Pagination
* Relationship Traversal
* N+1 Elimination

Benchmark Results:

Tested with Apidog. No application-level caching. PostgreSQL built-in query cache only. All datasets use fully randomized data.

Single Customer (eq filter):
* 5 datasets, 5 iterations, 10 assertions, 0 failures
* Average Response Time: 13 ms
* Max Response Time: 67 ms
* Total Duration: 239 ms
* Pass Rate: 100%

Three Customers (in filter, batch):
* 5 datasets, 5 iterations, 30 assertions, 0 failures
* Average Response Time: 16 ms
* Max Response Time: 78 ms
* Total Duration: 239 ms
* Pass Rate: 100%

Scaling from 1 to 3 customers added only 3 ms to average response time because all entities are resolved in a single batched SQL statement with no resolver chains or N+1 round trips.

Full benchmark details: BENCHMARKS.md

Target Audience:

* .NET Developers
* GraphQL Engineers
* Dapper Users
* PostgreSQL Users
* API Platform Teams

Status:

Active Development

Roadmap:

* Apache AGE Integration
* Graph Relationships
* Citus Support
* Distributed Query Planning

License:

MIT
